title: Hilary Gets A Life

jokes.text: And I thought it was tough being a pirate. It ain't nothing compared to being a salad wench.
jokes.setup: Tyriq mentions having to work double shifts at a restaurant
jokes.punchline: Comparing being a pirate (themed restaurant server) to a 'salad wench' as if they're equally legitimate historical occupations
jokes.explanation: Creative analogy between themed restaurant jobs and historical roles, with good wordplay on 'wench'

jokes.text: Maybe I should put my scent on it, huh? / Hey, I think not.
jokes.setup: Tyriq suggests putting his scent on his dating application
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate rejection implies Tyriq's scent would not help his dating prospects
jokes.explanation: Simple insult humor, well-delivered but predictable

jokes.text: Ty, you're not gonna get a date... you're gonna get a hot meal and a box of free clothes.
jokes.setup: Will reads Tyriq's honest but poor-sounding dating profile
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he'll attract charity rather than romance
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about how poverty-level income sounds in a dating context

jokes.text: Someone with really, really big breasts.
jokes.setup: After claiming to be a biblical scholar/nuclear physicist/doctor who saves orphans
jokes.punchline: Tyriq's ideal woman criteria is purely superficial
jokes.explanation: Predictable male character humor, relies on obvious contrast

jokes.text: I will never eat again. / So where are we going to lunch tomorrow?
jokes.setup: Hilary dramatically declares she'll never eat again
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks about tomorrow's lunch plans
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Hilary's shallow dramatic tendencies and food obsession

jokes.text: Well, when your looks go, they'll still respect you.
jokes.setup: Friend mentions studying for med school entrance exam
jokes.punchline: Hilary's backhanded compliment reveals her shallow worldview
jokes.explanation: Sharp character writing that reveals Hilary's values through seemingly supportive comment

jokes.text: You have trouble working the pumps on your shoes.
jokes.setup: Will argues no one will believe Tyriq is a nuclear physicist
jokes.punchline: Citing Tyriq's trouble with shoe pumps as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that contrasts complex physics with basic shoe operation

jokes.text: I see you as an extra from Hook.
jokes.setup: Tyriq asks if he looks more like a biblical scholar or nuclear physicist
jokes.punchline: Ashley sees him as a movie extra (referencing his pirate costume)
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture reference that deflates Tyriq's pretensions perfectly

jokes.text: You better watch it before I make you walk the plank.
jokes.setup: Ashley's Hook reference
jokes.punchline: Tyriq continues the pirate theme with threat
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that maintains the pirate character bit

jokes.text: Do I look like a Huxtable?
jokes.setup: Carlton complains about a family meeting
jokes.punchline: Reference to The Cosby Show's family meeting trope
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference to another NBC sitcom's family dynamics, very quotable

jokes.text: Is this some kind of sick joke? / Is this some kind of sick joke?
jokes.setup: Parents praise Will for being responsible
jokes.punchline: Will's disbelief repeated twice shows how unprecedented this is
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's shock at being praised, repetition adds emphasis

jokes.text: Are we, like, in the Twilight Zone? When does everyone put on the pig masks?
jokes.setup: Family continues praising Will unusually
jokes.punchline: Carlton references Twilight Zone episode with pig masks
jokes.explanation: Specific Twilight Zone reference that perfectly captures the surreal feeling

jokes.text: Honey, that's $9.
jokes.setup: Philip complains about Carlton's $90 socks
jokes.punchline: Vivian reveals it's actually $9, showing Philip's vision problems
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about Philip's vision, simple but effective

jokes.text: That's 80 cents. / Don't be ridiculous, Vivian.
jokes.setup: Philip complains about Ashley's $80 drug store charges
jokes.punchline: It's 80 cents, but Philip dismisses Vivian's correction
jokes.explanation: Continues the vision gag, Philip's stubbornness makes it funnier

jokes.text: If you move the TV any closer to the bed I'll be sleeping with Jay Leno.
jokes.setup: Philip insists he can see fine despite obvious evidence otherwise
jokes.punchline: Reveals the TV is practically in the bed due to his vision
jokes.explanation: Great visual image and perfect character moment showing Philip's denial

jokes.text: $300 dollars for shoes. / Point being?
jokes.setup: Philip reads what he thinks is an outrageous shoe bill
jokes.punchline: Hilary sees nothing wrong with $300 shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response showing her disconnect from normal spending concerns

jokes.text: I think I'll slide out and simonize my halo.
jokes.setup: Will realizes he's being praised and should leave before it ends
jokes.punchline: Claims to polish his halo like a car, mixing metaphors humorously
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing car maintenance with angelic imagery, very Will-like

jokes.text: You spend more on clothes than most small countries spend on grain.
jokes.setup: Philip lists Hilary's excessive spending
jokes.punchline: Compares her clothing budget to national food budgets
jokes.explanation: Strong hyperbolic comparison that effectively illustrates the excess

jokes.text: I'm making a New Year's resolution to find a job. Right after Easter.
jokes.setup: Hilary promises to make a New Year's resolution to get a job
jokes.punchline: Delays it until after Easter, defeating the purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her ability to procrastinate even promises

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late, I had to slide down to the nuclear facility and split an atom.
jokes.setup: Will pretending to be Dr. Smith at the dating service
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions splitting atoms like it's a quick errand
jokes.explanation: Good callback that treats nuclear physics as mundane, maintaining the absurd persona

jokes.text: I want my employer to want me for me, not for what I've accomplished.
jokes.setup: Hilary explaining why her interview went poorly
jokes.punchline: Wants to be hired despite having no qualifications or accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of privileged mindset, completely backwards from normal hiring

jokes.text: Yeah, Hil, I do and it scares the hell out of me.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks if Will understands her backwards hiring philosophy
jokes.punchline: Will's honest horror at actually understanding her logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that amplifies the absurdity of Hilary's thinking

jokes.text: What? Your body? / No, my résumé.
jokes.setup: Hilary says employers only want 'one thing'
jokes.punchline: Will assumes something sexual, but it's just her résumé
jokes.explanation: Predictable misdirection relying on sexual implication, fairly standard

jokes.text: You're gonna have to stop dotting your I's with these little hearts.
jokes.setup: Will looking at Hilary's résumé for improvements
jokes.punchline: Points out her childish résumé formatting
jokes.explanation: Great visual detail that shows Hilary's complete lack of professional awareness

jokes.text: I don't know, 4 maybe 500. / Okay, there we go. 6,000 hours of catering experience.
jokes.setup: Will asks how many catered affairs Hilary has attended
jokes.punchline: Will inflates 'maybe 500' parties into professional experience hours
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's creative résumé writing and Hilary's party attendance

jokes.text: Yes. I gained 10 pounds eating croissants. / Studied pastry making with the French masters.
jokes.setup: Will asks about Hilary's summers in Paris
jokes.punchline: Will spins weight gain from eating into professional pastry training
jokes.explanation: Clever transformation of personal indulgence into professional qualification

jokes.text: I burned the entire stove to a crisp. / Expert in the art of flambé.
jokes.setup: Hilary admits to burning down a stove while cooking
jokes.punchline: Will reframes the disaster as flambé expertise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reframing of kitchen disaster as culinary skill, perfect wordplay with flambé

jokes.text: You got a broom? I mean, I could go bang on the ceiling.
jokes.setup: Ashley suggests Tyriq wasn't loud enough calling for Will
jokes.punchline: Tyriq proposes even more primitive communication method
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation humor, nothing special but functional

jokes.text: I got the shoulders for it. / That shirt looks good on anyone. All the guys in the eighth grade have one.
jokes.setup: Tyriq brags about having the right build for the shirt
jokes.punchline: Will deflates his ego by revealing it's a common eighth-grade shirt
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of masculine posturing with age-inappropriate clothing reference

jokes.text: You know, I better return mine before my scent overpowers it.
jokes.setup: Tyriq decides to return his shirt too
jokes.punchline: References his earlier scent comment self-deprecatingly
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows character growth through self-awareness

jokes.text: I think you guys are spending too much time together.
jokes.setup: Will and Tyriq acting increasingly similar
jokes.punchline: Ashley's deadpan observation about their co-dependency
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor from the youngest character seeing the obvious

jokes.text: Please. You can't afford me.
jokes.setup: Vivian suggests Hilary help with dinner to practice cooking
jokes.punchline: Hilary treats family dinner as beneath her professional rate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary line showing her instant transformation into a prima donna professional

jokes.text: I deserve to keep the shirt because I was in eighth grade the longest.
jokes.setup: Tyriq and Will arguing over who returns their matching shirt
jokes.punchline: Tyriq claims seniority based on repeating eighth grade
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic turning academic failure into a qualification for keeping child's clothing

jokes.text: Come on, this definitely ain't gonna do nothing for the misconception that all brothers look alike.
jokes.setup: Will and Tyriq wearing identical shirts
jokes.punchline: Will acknowledges the racial stereotype implications
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary disguised as fashion humor, addresses stereotypes cleverly

jokes.text: It's 'bar mitzvah,' dunce cap. The Bar Mista is a go-go joint on Sunset.
jokes.setup: Tyriq mispronounces 'bar mitzvah' as 'Bar Mista'
jokes.punchline: Will corrects him and explains what 'Bar Mista' would actually be
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that creates a completely different establishment from the mispronunciation

jokes.text: I don't want to miss out on a lovely young lady who thinks I'm Arsenio's younger brother, Vesuvia.
jokes.setup: Tyriq explaining why he needs to leave for his date
jokes.punchline: His dating profile apparently claims he's related to Arsenio Hall with a made-up name
jokes.explanation: Fantastic absurd lie with the made-up name 'Vesuvia' being particularly funny

jokes.text: By that you mean, the crew that serves? / By that you mean, the room where the party is?
jokes.setup: Barb asks about the serving crew location
jokes.punchline: Hilary stalls by defining obvious terms, revealing she has no crew
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Hilary's panic and inexperience through obvious stalling

jokes.text: What a great idea, Hillary, I love the matching shirts. My son in eighth grade has one.
jokes.setup: Will and Tyriq reluctantly serving in their eighth-grade shirts
jokes.punchline: Client compliments the 'professional' look, unknowingly referencing the age appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that creates ironic praise for their inappropriate attire

jokes.text: Do I look like Hazel?
jokes.setup: Hilary asks if Carlton can stay and help serve
jokes.punchline: Carlton references the classic TV show about a maid
jokes.explanation: Good reference that shows Carlton's knowledge of classic TV and class consciousness

jokes.text: That's cool. Just make sure Uncle Phil pays you top dollar for it.
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions selling her hair as backup plan
jokes.punchline: Will references Uncle Phil's obvious need for hair
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback about Philip's baldness, practical solution to Hilary's plan

jokes.text: What kind of monster are you?
jokes.setup: Will refuses to help his sister despite her tears
jokes.punchline: Ashley's moral judgment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Ashley's innocent moral clarity cuts through Will's selfishness effectively

jokes.text: I'd rather share a seesaw with Delta Burke.
jokes.setup: Will asks Carlton to help serve at the party
jokes.punchline: Carlton uses exaggerated comparison to actress known for weight struggles
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference that relies on weight-based humor, less clever than other jokes

jokes.text: You know how hard it is for me to say no? No. Easier than I thought. Neat.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims it's hard for him to refuse requests
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates how easy it actually is for him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment where Carlton discovers his own character trait in real time

jokes.text: Sort of like that heat rash on your neck that you've been passing off in gym class as a hickey?
jokes.setup: Carlton lectures about lies coming back to haunt you
jokes.punchline: Reveals Will's embarrassing lie about his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeback that deflates Will's moral high ground with embarrassing personal detail

jokes.text: 13-year-old boys? Count me in. After all, Hillary's my sister.
jokes.setup: Will mentions the party is for 13-year-old boys
jokes.punchline: Carlton suddenly becomes interested when he hears the age demographic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's immaturity and age-inappropriate interests

jokes.text: How much fun could you have with chumpies named Di, Fergie, and Elizabeth?
jokes.setup: Will dismisses the party as boring
jokes.punchline: Uses names that sound common but are actually British royalty
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection using royal names as if they were common, appealing to Geoffrey's British background

jokes.text: It's clearly a lie. It's a blatant, bold-faced lie! I'll hate myself in the morning.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey recognizes Will's manipulation about royal names
jokes.punchline: Despite knowing it's a lie, he can't resist the British royal connection
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Geoffrey's weakness for anything British despite his better judgment

jokes.text: In front of all these people?
jokes.setup: Will explains a bar mitzvah is when a Jewish boy becomes a man
jokes.punchline: Carlton misunderstands the ceremonial nature
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo based on Carlton's innocent misunderstanding of the ceremony

jokes.text: As for you, there is a special place for you in homeboy hell.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey graciously agrees to help Hilary
jokes.punchline: Threatens Will with street-specific damnation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey line mixing proper British diction with urban slang

jokes.text: this is my one chance to date a woman who doesn't wear all her gold in her mouth.
jokes.setup: Tyriq reluctantly agrees to help but wants to leave soon
jokes.punchline: Reveals his usual dating demographic through dental jewelry reference
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Tyriq's dating history, specific and visual

jokes.text: Say it once, say it loud, we've got brie and we're proud!
jokes.setup: Hilary trying to motivate her makeshift serving team
jokes.punchline: Parodies 'Say it loud, I'm black and proud' with cheese reference
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of the James Brown song adapted for catering context

jokes.text: Well, Hillary, other than the pork thing, they're pretty much just like us.
jokes.setup: Hilary expresses fear about 'them' being out there
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's matter-of-fact comparison addressing kosher dietary laws
jokes.explanation: Good cultural humor that deflates othering while acknowledging real differences

jokes.text: Hillary, don't be ridiculous. Carlton's the laughingstock of Bel-Air.
jokes.setup: Hilary worries about being the laughingstock if seen serving
jokes.punchline: Will reassures her that position is already taken by Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good sibling insult that serves plot function of reassuring Hilary

jokes.text: Blacks and Jews have a lot in common. Cherished heritage, a strong sense of community... frizzy hair.
jokes.setup: Will making serious cultural observations
jokes.punchline: Ends with superficial physical trait
jokes.explanation: Thoughtful cultural observation that deflates into physical comedy

jokes.text: Oh, look, a Donna Karan suit right in the middle of the kitchen floor! What color?
jokes.setup: Will tries to lure fashion-obsessed Hilary out with fake designer clothing
jokes.punchline: Hilary takes the bait despite knowing it's manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific manipulation that shows Hilary's fashion obsession overriding logic

jokes.text: Sonny, what you do in the privacy of the bedroom is nobody's business but yours. Just be true to yourself! He'll come around.
jokes.setup: Will's phrasing about coming out of the closet
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey misinterprets as coming out sexually, offers supportive response
jokes.explanation: Standard misunderstanding humor based on double meaning of 'coming out'

jokes.text: Maybe in Des Moines. This is L.A., okay?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey suggests self-worth matters more than others' opinions
jokes.punchline: Hilary dismisses this as geographic naivety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response showing her belief that superficiality is location-specific

jokes.text: I quit. I had to, it irritated my chin.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks why Hilary quit violin after begging for lessons
jokes.punchline: Absurdly superficial reason for abandoning musical pursuit
jokes.explanation: Shows Hilary's pattern of quitting for trivial aesthetic reasons

jokes.text: I was starting to get feet like Fred Flintstone.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks why she quit ballet
jokes.punchline: Hilary worried about developing cartoon character feet
jokes.explanation: Funny visual image mixing real body concern with cartoon reference

jokes.text: But they wanted me to go to away games on a bus!
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks why she quit cheerleading
jokes.punchline: Hilary treats bus travel as unacceptable hardship
jokes.explanation: Shows Hilary's privileged horror at normal teenage experiences

jokes.text: She can sculpt cauliflower into a bust of Barbra Streisand!
jokes.setup: Hilary imagines how her friend might introduce her catering skills
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd food sculpture reference
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd skill that sounds both impressive and ridiculous

jokes.text: Geoffrey don't raise no fools.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey claims he helped raise Hilary well
jokes.punchline: Uses non-standard grammar while claiming competence
jokes.explanation: Great character moment where Geoffrey adopts informal speech pattern for emphasis

jokes.text: But she's so dowdy. Can I be Princess Caroline instead?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey tells her to enter like the Queen of England
jokes.punchline: Hilary prefers a more fashionable royal
jokes.explanation: Shows Hilary's fashion-first thinking even when choosing royal role models

jokes.text: Now you get your spoiled butt up out of this closet and you go out there and you strut your puffs!
jokes.setup: Will finally loses patience with Hilary's hiding
jokes.punchline: Mixes 'strut your stuff' with pastry reference
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that connects the catering context with motivational speaking

jokes.text: I'm a bad boy, ain't I?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey gets satisfaction from his tough love approach
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's pleased self-assessment of his unusual behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment showing rare satisfaction with being unconventional

tmdb_overview: Hilary must work for a caterer after her credit cards are cancelled.
